_source: AWS Welding Handbook, CWI certification materials, Fabrication shop standards
_note: Long-tail welding data. Process parameters, electrode selection, joint prep, defect identification.

smaw_stick.process: Consumable electrode with flux coating; manual; DC or AC
smaw_stick.common_applications: Construction, pipeline, repair, field work, structural steel

gmaw_mig.process: Continuous wire electrode; shielding gas; semi-automatic

short_circuit.characteristics: Wire touches puddle; short circuits 50-200x/sec; low heat; thin material
short_circuit.applications: Sheet metal, auto body, thin-wall tubing

spray_transfer.characteristics: Wire never touches puddle; axial droplets; requires 85%+ argon; smooth, spatter-free
spray_transfer.applications: Thick material, flat/horizontal only, high-quality welds

pulse_spray.characteristics: Alternates high and low current; spray transfer at lower average current; all positions

globular.characteristics: Large, erratic droplets; high spatter; avoid if possible; occurs with CO₂ or low voltage
globular.applications: None preferred; transition between short circuit and spray

steel.100_co2: Cheapest; deep penetration; high spatter; shop fabrication
steel.75ar_25co2: Most common; balanced penetration and spatter; C25
steel.90ar_10co2: Spray transfer; cleaner; stainless and critical welds

aluminum.100_argon: Standard for aluminum; spray or pulse modes only

stainless.tri_mix: He/Ar/CO₂; high heat input; thick stainless

gtaw_tig.process: Non-consumable tungsten electrode; separate filler rod; inert gas shielding

electrode_grind.dcen: Grind to sharp point (20-30° included angle); lengthwise grinding for stable arc
electrode_grind.ac: Balled end; electrode end forms ball during arc; 1/8" electrode = 1/8" ball

shielding_gas.argon: Standard for all TIG; 100% argon most common
shielding_gas.helium_mix: 75Ar/25He; hotter arc; faster travel on aluminum or thick material
shielding_gas.argon_hydrogen: Ar/H₂ for stainless; hotter, faster; austenitic stainless only (not carbon steel)

ac_vs_dc.dcen: Electrode negative; deep penetration; steel, stainless, copper
ac_vs_dc.dcep: Electrode positive; shallow penetration; rarely used; severe tungsten erosion
ac_vs_dc.ac: Alternates DCEN/DCEP; cleaning action on aluminum oxide; use for aluminum

fcaw_flux_core.process: Tubular wire filled with flux; gas-shielded or self-shielded
fcaw_flux_core.self_shielded_fcaw: No external gas; flux creates shielding; outdoor/field work; structural steel
fcaw_flux_core.gas_shielded_fcaw: Uses CO₂ or 75/25; higher deposition rate than MIG; less spatter than self-shielded
fcaw_flux_core.applications: Shipbuilding, heavy fabrication, structural steel, high-production welding

butt_joint.square_groove: No bevel; material ≤1/8"; full penetration with backing or from both sides
butt_joint.single_v: 60° included angle; 1/8" root gap; 1/16" land; material 1/8" to 3/4"
butt_joint.double_v: Both sides beveled; thick material >3/4"; reduces filler metal; minimizes distortion
butt_joint.single_bevel: One plate beveled; backup plate on other side; 45° bevel angle

fillet_weld.tee_joint: 90° plates; fillet weld both sides for strength
fillet_weld.leg_size: Fillet size; 1/4" fillet = 1/4" legs; throat = leg × 0.707

edge_preparation.grinding: Remove mill scale, rust, paint, oil; clean to bare metal

fit_up_tolerance.alignment: Within 10% of thickness offset; excessive offset = stress concentration

current_settings_mig.wire_feed_speed: Higher WFS = higher current; 250-400 IPM typical range
current_settings_mig.voltage_effect: Higher voltage = wider, flatter bead; lower voltage = narrower, higher crown

travel_speed.too_fast: Narrow bead, undercut, lack of fusion, wormhole appearance
travel_speed.too_slow: Wide bead, excessive buildup, spatter, burned edges, slag inclusions
travel_speed.correct_speed: Bead width 2-3× electrode diameter; uniform ripples; good tie-in at toes

arc_length.short_arc: Electrode close to puddle; e6010/e6011 drag the rod; 7018 short arc 1/16" to 1/8"
arc_length.long_arc: Excessive arc length; spatter, porosity, weak weld; maintain proper distance

porosity.appearance: Gas holes in weld; rounded cavities
causes: Moisture in flux or on base metal
porosity.prevention: Clean base metal; dry electrodes; increase gas flow (20-25 CFM); block wind

undercut.appearance: Groove melted into base metal at toe of weld; not filled by weld metal
undercut.prevention: Reduce current; shorten arc; slow down; adjust angle to 10-15° from perpendicular

lack_of_fusion.appearance: Weld metal did not fuse to base metal or previous pass
lack_of_fusion.detection: Crack-like indication on RT or UT; destructive bend test shows separation
lack_of_fusion.prevention: Increase current; slow down; clean joint faces; manipulate to wet out edges

lack_of_penetration.appearance: Weld did not penetrate through full thickness
lack_of_penetration.prevention: Increase current; open root gap to 1/8"; grind land to 1/16"; back-gouge and weld from both sides

causes: Trapped slag in undercut or overlap
slag_inclusions.prevention: Wire brush and chip slag completely; inspect before next pass; use proper technique

cracks.hot_cracks: Form during solidification; centerline or crater cracks; caused by high sulfur/carbon, restraint
cracks.cold_cracks: Form after cooling; hydrogen-induced; high-carbon steel; thermal stress
cracks.prevention: Preheat high-carbon steel; use low-hydrogen electrodes (7018); post-weld heat treatment; reduce restraint

visual.tools: Weld gauge, fillet gauge, straight edge, magnifier

dye_penetrant.process: Apply penetrant; wait 10-30 min; clean; apply developer; indications show surface cracks
dye_penetrant.use: Non-ferrous metals, welds, castings; detects surface-breaking defects only

magnetic_particle.process: Magnetize part; apply iron particles; defects disrupt magnetic field; particles concentrate at cracks
magnetic_particle.use: Ferrous metals only; surface and slightly subsurface cracks

radiographic.process: X-ray or gamma ray through weld; film or digital detector; shadows show internal defects
radiographic.detects: Porosity, inclusions, lack of fusion, cracks, incomplete penetration
radiographic.limitations: Expensive, slow, radiation safety requirements, crack orientation matters

ultrasonic.process: Ultrasonic transducer sends sound waves; reflections from defects detected
ultrasonic.advantages: Fast, portable, no radiation, detects deep defects
ultrasonic.limitations: Requires skilled operator, calibration, couplant, surface finish affects results

carbon_content.low_carbon: <0.3% C; mild steel; easy to weld; no preheat needed; 1018, A36
carbon_content.high_carbon: >0.6% C; preheat 400-600°F; difficult to weld; crack-sensitive; tool steel

heat_affected_zone.definition: Base metal adjacent to weld; heated but not melted; microstructure changed
heat_affected_zone.grain_growth: High heat causes grain growth; reduced toughness; minimize with lower heat input
heat_affected_zone.hardening: High-carbon steel hardens in HAZ; becomes brittle; preheat and slow cooling prevents

weld_metal_matching.undermatching: Weld weaker than base metal; acceptable for ductile materials; weld yields first
weld_metal_matching.matching: Weld strength equals base metal; most common
weld_metal_matching.overmatching: Weld stronger than base metal; HAZ becomes weak point; common practice for safety margin
